open_gaps.SEC-002: LOW (unchanged). Still no numPages>1000 cap at viewer.js loadDocument() (the single viewer-load boundary) and no cumulative-queued-bytes cap at the multi-file ingest boundaries (merge.js queued PDFs, img2pdf.js queued images). This tick's two new modules (sanitize.js TASK-365, image-manager.js TASK-366) BOTH touch ONLY the already-validated open doc via getData()+pdf-lib and upload/mutate-the-viewer-doc nothing -> NEITHER is a new ingest boundary. Same single fix (page-count cap once at viewer load + cumulative-bytes caps in merge.js/img2pdf.js addFiles()). Developer task, not security's to implement.
open_gaps.SEC-JS-INFLATE: LOW (unchanged from prior tick). js-inspector.js (TASK-364) inflates FlateDecode /JS streams FULLY into memory via DecompressionStream before truncating, and collectNameTree has no cap on script-entry count (only depth-50 guard). Self-inflicted single-tab amplifier on the 1.6 GiB box. NOTE: sanitize.js (TASK-365) does NOT add to this gap -- it only COUNTS name-tree leaves (countNameTreeLeaves, depth-50 guard) and deletes the subtree, never inflating stream bytes; it is in fact the remediation a user runs to remove embedded JS. Recommended developer hardening (on js-inspector.js): cap inflated bytes (abort stream past ~1-2 MB) + cap script-entry count.
open_gaps.SEC-CSP-FRAME: INFO. frame-src 'self' blob: in the live CSP (added 2026-06-13 for the Print tool). Allows only same-origin + same-origin-blob frames, no remote origins; frame-ancestors 'self' unchanged. Verified live this tick.

notes: 57 JS modules (+2 since the 2026-06-14 08:30 review: sanitize.js TASK-365, image-manager.js TASK-366). BOTH CLEAN, no new XSS/exec/network findings. sanitize.js is a MUTATE->DOWNLOAD tool (burst.js/split.js class) that strips /Names /JavaScript, auto-actions (/OpenAction + doc & page /AA) and /Names /EmbeddedFiles from a FRESH pdf-lib copy of the open doc's getData() bytes (ignoreEncryption:true, updateMetadata:false) and downloads the cleaned PDF -- it touches ONLY the already-validated open doc, never mutates the viewer doc, uploads nothing -> NOT a new ingest boundary, no separate cap. Pure structural catalog deletes (no rasterization/lib/network). It is the remediation half of js-inspector and notably does NOT add to SEC-JS-INFLATE (counts leaves only, never inflates streams). Filename sanitized [^a-zA-Z0-9._-]->_ slice(180) -> <base>_sanitized.pdf; status+per-category summary via textContent only (no innerHTML); shared zip-writer downloadBytes; loadToken stale guard; no-doc/PDFLib-unready guarded; errors -> EventBus.ERROR. image-manager.js is a READ-ONLY image XObject catalog (dims/bit-depth/colorspace/filter/SMask/page-refs) -- touches ONLY the open doc (getData()+pdf-lib structural read), decodes/renders/downloads/uploads NOTHING (NOT an ingest boundary; SEC-002 per-page consumer family; Form XObject recursion capped at depth 1 with visited ref-set cycle guard). XSS-safe: PDF-supplied colorspace/filter names via textContent/addRow only (innerHTML only for empty-string list clears). New-file sink audit: zero real innerHTML(non-empty)/outerHTML/insertAdjacentHTML/document.write/eval/new Function/fetch/XHR/sendBeacon/<a href> across both; zero external requests in both + app.js.
